news.title: Publication: Summary of Deliberations
news.summary:
13:30 (ET)
A summary of monetary policy deliberations by the Governing Council for the policy decision that was announced two weeks earlier.

news.title: Interest Rate Announcement
news.summary:
09:45 (ET)
On eight scheduled dates each year, the Bank of Canada announces the setting for the overnight rate target in a press release explaining the factors behind the decision.

news.title: Remembrance Day
news.summary: National holiday

news.title: Release: Market Participants Survey
news.summary:
10:30 (ET)
The Market Participants Survey is conducted quarterly. Bank of Canada staff reach out to a diverse set of participants in financial markets to gather their views on key macroeconomic and financial variables as well as on monetary policy.

news.summary:

The Business Outlook Survey is a summary of interviews conducted by the Bank's regional offices with business leaders from about 100 firms, selected in accordance with the composition of Canada's gross domestic product. The Canadian Survey of Consumer Expectations i...

news.title: Major US stock indices close lower. S&P and NASDAQ index down for the month
news.summary: Both the S&P index only NASDAQ index closed lower for the month. The S&P fell -0.87% after a gain of 1.37% in the month of January. The NASDAQ index-3.38%. That was its worst month since March 2025. The Dow industrial average rose by a modest 0.17%. For the trading day...

news.title: Gold hits $5,260 as war jitters, trade tension rattle markets
news.summary: A red-hot inflation report in the United States and rising tensions between the latter and Iran pushed Gold price higher on Friday, past the $5,260 figure, posting solid gains of over 1.20%.

news.title: Week Ahead: US Dollar slips on trade uncertainty as NFP, Eurozone HICP loom
news.summary: The US Dollar (USD) lost ground this week amid geopolitical uncertainty and the United States (US) trade policy developments after the Supreme Court ruled the Trump administration's tariffs illegal and he responded with a fresh round of levies.

news.title: Taiwan: Data to confirm strong upswing – DBS
news.summary: DBS Group Research expects Taiwan’s upcoming January export orders and industrial production to show strong double-digit growth, helped by last year’s low base and robust global AI-driven demand, alongside semiconductor and ICT inventory restocking.

news.title: DXY slips as hot PPI stokes stagflation fears
news.summary: DXY fell about 0.2% on Friday, grinding back into 97.60 after a hotter-than-expected Producer Price Index (PPI) report amplified concerns that inflation is proving stickier than the Federal Reserve (Fed) would like, weighing on growth expectations and dragging the Greenback lo...

news.title: India: Weak growth impulse and fiscal risks – Societe Generale
news.summary: Societe Generale’s Kunal Kundu reviews India’s FY27 Union Budget, highlighting policy continuity and fiscal consolidation in a context of geopolitical strains and currency weakness.

news.title: US Dollar Weekly Forecast: At a crossroads; Fed steady, tariffs in flux
news.summary: The US Dollar’s (USD) upward momentum from the previous week seems to have encountered a tough nut to crack in the 98.00 region, as measured by the US Dollar Index (DXY).

news.title: GBP/USD slips as US PPI lifts US Dollar, Middle East risks rise
news.summary: The Pound Sterling (GBP) drops some 0.10% on Friday as the Greenback is underpinned by a hot inflation report in the US, which prompted investors to price in a less dovish Federal Reserve (Fed).

news.title: AUD/USD holds firm as US Dollar retreats despite firm PPI
news.summary: The Australian Dollar (AUD) trades flat against the US Dollar (USD) on Friday as the Greenback reverses earlier gains despite stronger-than-expected US Producer Price Index (PPI) data. At the time of writing, AUD/USD is trading around 0.7112 and is on track for an eighth conse...

news.title: China: Policy mix guides 2026 growth path – UOB
news.summary: UOB’s Ho Woei Chen expects China’s National People’s Congress to set a 2026 real GDP growth target of 4.5–5.0%, with actual growth forecast at 4.7%.

news.title: Indonesia: Inflation seen higher on base effects – DBS
news.summary: DBS Group Research expects Indonesia’s February inflation to rise to 4.1% year-on-year, driven by a low base and fading one-off stimulus in administered prices. While most components should stay subdued, elevated precious metal prices are seen lifting personal care costs.

news.title: Trump: Not happy with Iran but more talks expected on Friday
news.summary: US Pres. Trump is speaking on Iran and says:Not happy with Iran, but were talks expected Friday.Has not made a decision on Iran and not happy with how they negotiate.Iran cannot have nuclear weapon.There may or may not be a regime change in Iran.Wants to make a deal with Iran....

news.title: Atlanta Fed GDPNow growth estimate for Q1 3.0% versus 3.1% last
news.summary: The Atlanta Fed GDPNow growth estimate for Q1 dipped to 3.0% from 3.1% last. In their own wordsThe GDPNow model estimate for real GDP growth (seasonally adjusted annual rate) in the first quarter of 2026 is 3.0 percent on February 27, down from 3.1 percent on February 24. Afte...

news.title: Asia FX: Oil shock risks as US–Iran talks progress – MUFG
news.summary: MUFG’s Senior Currency Analyst Lloyd Chan highlights that an escalation in US–Iran tensions could trigger an Oil price shock, reviving global inflation and hurting Asia’s net Oil importers.

news.title: NZD/USD advances as US Dollar softens, supported by RBNZ growth outlook
news.summary: NZD/USD trades around 0.6000 on Friday at the time of writing, up 0.19% on the day, benefiting from a modest pullback in the US Dollar (USD) amid a wait-and-see mood ahead of fresh signals on the outlook for monetary policy in the United States (US).

news.title: Dow Jones Industrial Average drops 600 points as hot PPI data rattles markets
news.summary: The Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 600 points, or 1.15%, tumbling back below 49,000 on Friday, capping off a turbulent final trading week in February. The S&P 500 dropped around 0.7% while the Nasdaq Composite lost roughly 0.9%.

news.title: Iran strikes loom large over today's trade
news.summary: There is no sense over-analyzing price action today across markets. Yes, there is the usual software anxiety in stock markets but the overarching theme across bonds, FX, commodities and precious metals is simple -- angst about US strikes on Iran.That thinking has US 10-year yi...

news.title: UK: Data and surveys preview – Deutsche Bank
news.summary: Deutsche Bank outlines a busy UK data calendar, expecting stronger Net Consumer Credit, a partial rebound in Mortgage Approvals to 64.5k, and unchanged PMI Manufacturing and Services at 52.0 and 53.9.

news.title: Turkey: Growth resilience and inflation risks – ING
news.summary: ING’s Muhammet Mercan projects Turkey’s 4Q25 GDP growth at 3.9%, implying 3.8% for 2025, underpinned by domestic demand but with some quarterly loss of momentum.

news.title: EUR/USD Weekly Forecast: Fed calm, ECB steady, but the Dollar still leads
news.summary: EUR/USD is still struggling to find real traction. The pair has tried to stabilise, but momentum keeps fading, leaving the door open to further weakness.

news.title: GBP/USD Weekly Forecast: Will Pound Sterling defend key 1.3450 support ahead of US jobs data?
news.summary: The Pound Sterling (GBP) entered a bearish consolidation phase against the US Dollar (USD), after having tested critical support near the 1.3450 level on several occasions.

news.title: EUR/USD steadies as strong US PPI data fails to lift Dollar
news.summary: EUR/USD steadies on Friday, extending the range-bound price action that has defined trading so far this week. The Euro (EUR) remains relatively firm after the US Dollar (USD) failed to build on stronger-than-expected US Producer Price Index (PPI) data.

news.title: Gold Weekly Forecast: Falling US yields, geopolitics help XAU/USD hold ground
news.summary: Gold (XAU/USD) gained traction and climbed above $5,200, ending the fourth consecutive week in positive territory. The next round of US-Iran talks and crucial macroeconomic data releases from the US will be watched closely by market participants in the short term.

news.title: USD/JPY slips as US PPI beats expectations, BoJ tightening bolsters Yen
news.summary: USD/JPY hovers around 156.00 on Friday at the time of writing, down 0.08% on the day, despite the release of stronger-than-expected US Producer Price Index (PPI) data. The move reflects a measured market reaction, with the US Dollar (USD) struggling to extend its rebound after...

news.title: Sweden: Gradual improvement with elevated unemployment – Nordea
news.summary: Nordea’s Anna Westlund reports that Swedish employment has been rising since early 2025, with BAS and LFS data both showing a positive trend.

news.title: Gold rises above $5,200 as US-Iran tensions bolster safe-haven appeal
news.summary: Gold (XAU/USD) trades slightly higher on Friday, pressing against the upper boundary of this week’s established range, after reports that US officials advised nonessential embassy staff in Israel to depart amid rising tensions, stoking fears of potential military escalation in...

news.title: MXN: Policy easing signals and resilient currency – Societe Generale
news.summary: Societe Generale analysts note that Deputy Governor Galia Borja signaled Banxico has room to resume rate cuts, pointing to weak domestic demand, falling investment and a stronger Peso.

news.title: Silver surges past $91 as hot PPI, tariffs stoke inflation fears
news.summary: Silver price (XAG/USD) extends its gains for the third consecutive day on Friday after the latest inflation report in the United States prompted investors to seek the safety of the white metal, while the Greenback remained firm. At the time of writing, XAG/USD trades near $91....

news.title: US December construction spending +0.3% vs +0.3% expected
news.summary: Prior was +0.5%Private construction +0.5%Residential construction +1.5%Non-residential construction -0.7%Public construction -0.5% m/mPublic construction +3.6% y/yGiven the jumps in shares of CAT and DE, there better be some construction spending in the pipeline. The annualize...

news.title: Oil: Geopolitics keep prices supported – Commerzbank
news.summary: Commerzbank’s Carsten Fritsch expects OPEC+ to only slightly increase Oil production from April, with Russia underproducing and Kazakhstan constrained, so a modest quota hike should not pressure prices.
